domains.title: Archivo de ceramica de Puerto Claro

concepts.title: Ingreso de piezas ceramicas
concepts.description: Criterios para recibir piezas y documentar su estado inicial sin intervenirlas.

sections.heading: Revision del embalaje
sections.text: El embalaje se examina antes de mover la pieza. Las deformaciones, zonas humedas y particulas sueltas se anotan mientras el objeto permanece apoyado en su base de transporte.

sections.heading: Alta inicial
sections.text: Cada ingreso recibe un codigo temporal, una fotografia general y una descripcion breve del estado visible. Una fractura activa o un residuo desconocido mantiene la pieza en observacion.

concepts.title: Limpieza superficial controlada
concepts.description: Metodo conservador para retirar polvo suelto de superficies ceramicas estables.

sections.heading: Trabajo en seco
sections.text: El polvo se retira con una brocha de fibra blanda y aspiracion indirecta a baja potencia. La boquilla conserva una separacion minima de ocho centimetros respecto de la superficie.

sections.heading: Condiciones de exclusion
sections.text: No se aplica humedad sobre engobes pulverulentos, sales visibles ni pigmentos que se desprenden al contacto leve. En esos casos la actividad se detiene y se conserva el residuo recuperado.

concepts.title: Estabilizacion temporal de fragmentos
concepts.description: Preparacion reversible de fragmentos para su estudio y almacenamiento seguro.

sections.heading: Ensayo de ajuste
sections.text: Los fragmentos se aproximan sobre espuma neutra sin aplicar adhesivo. La posicion se considera compatible cuando los bordes coinciden sin presion y no quedan puntos de carga concentrada.

sections.heading: Proteccion de bordes
sections.text: Los bordes fragiles se separan con tiras de tejido inerte. Las piezas menores de tres centimetros se guardan en cavidades individuales para evitar movimientos durante la manipulacion.

concepts.title: Registro de pigmentos y decoraciones
concepts.description: Condiciones de observacion para describir colores, trazos y perdidas decorativas.

sections.heading: Iluminacion de observacion
sections.text: La inspeccion utiliza luz difusa de 5000 K y evita radiacion ultravioleta directa. La pieza permanece en la misma orientacion durante toda la secuencia fotografica.

sections.heading: Referencia cromatica
sections.text: Una carta de color neutra se incluye en la primera imagen de cada cara. Los cambios de tono se describen con palabras y nunca se deducen unicamente de una fotografia.

concepts.title: Clima del deposito ceramico
concepts.description: Rangos ambientales y respuesta basica ante variaciones del deposito.

sections.heading: Rangos de conservacion
sections.text: La humedad relativa se mantiene entre 42 % y 55 %, con variaciones diarias menores de 5 puntos. La temperatura operativa permanece entre 18 C y 23 C.

sections.heading: Desvios persistentes
sections.text: Una lectura fuera de rango durante mas de dos horas activa una inspeccion de estanterias y envoltorios. Los objetos no se trasladan mientras exista condensacion visible.

concepts.title: Embalaje para reserva
concepts.description: Materiales y separaciones usados para conservar piezas ceramicas dentro del deposito.

sections.heading: Materiales admitidos
sections.text: Las cajas emplean carton neutro, espuma de polietileno cerrada y tejido sin apresto. No se permiten grapas expuestas, lana mineral ni plasticos con olor persistente.

sections.heading: Separacion interna
sections.text: La pieza conserva dos centimetros de amortiguacion en sus caras estables y cuatro en zonas salientes. Ningun fragmento comparte cavidad con otro objeto.

concepts.title: Preparacion para salida a exposicion
concepts.description: Controles aplicados antes de transportar una pieza hacia una sala temporal.

sections.heading: Control de condicion
sections.text: La superficie, las uniones y las zonas decoradas se comparan con imagenes recientes. Cualquier perdida nueva suspende la salida hasta que se determine su estabilidad.

sections.heading: Condiciones de transito
sections.text: La caja viaja en posicion indicada, protegida de lluvia y con registro de impactos. El periodo continuo de traslado no supera seis horas sin una parada de control.

concepts.title: Retorno de piezas prestadas
concepts.description: Recepcion, observacion y cierre de movimientos despues de una exposicion externa.

sections.heading: Periodo de observacion
sections.text: La caja cerrada se estabiliza durante cuatro horas en una zona intermedia. Luego se comprueban polvo, humedad, desplazamientos y cambios visibles en la pieza.

sections.heading: Cierre del movimiento
sections.text: El movimiento termina cuando las fotografias de retorno, las lecturas ambientales y las observaciones estan completas. Una diferencia sin explicacion mantiene abierto el seguimiento.

concepts.title: Recepcion de materiales organicos
concepts.description: Criterios para aceptar aportes vegetales y separar materiales impropios.

sections.heading: Aportes admitidos
sections.text: Se aceptan restos de frutas, verduras, hojas secas y poda triturada sin tratamiento quimico reciente. Las bolsas se vacian y retiran antes de pesar el aporte.

sections.heading: Materiales rechazados
sections.text: Carne, aceite, vidrio, metales y residuos sanitarios no ingresan a las pilas. Un aporte con olor quimico o presencia abundante de plastico queda aislado.

concepts.title: Balance de la mezcla inicial
concepts.description: Proporciones practicas para combinar materiales humedos y secos.

sections.heading: Proporcion de partida
sections.text: La mezcla comienza con dos recipientes de material seco por cada recipiente de material humedo. La poda gruesa se tritura hasta piezas menores de cinco centimetros.

sections.heading: Textura esperada
sections.text: Una porcion comprimida debe conservar su forma sin liberar agua. Si se desarma de inmediato se agrega material humedo; si gotea se incorpora material seco.

concepts.title: Control de humedad de las pilas
concepts.description: Comprobaciones y ajustes de humedad durante el proceso de compostaje.

sections.heading: Medicion semanal
sections.text: La humedad se comprueba a veinte centimetros de profundidad en tres puntos. El rango operativo es de 45 % a 60 %.

sections.heading: Ajustes permitidos
sections.text: Por debajo del rango se anade agua en capas finas durante el volteo. Por encima del rango se agregan hojas secas y se mejora la ventilacion lateral.

concepts.title: Seguimiento de temperatura
concepts.description: Lectura del nucleo y respuesta ante temperaturas anomalas.

sections.heading: Lectura del nucleo
sections.text: La sonda permanece cinco minutos en el centro de la pila. Durante la fase activa se esperan valores entre 50 C y 65 C.

sections.heading: Respuesta a valores extremos
sections.text: Una lectura superior a 68 C requiere volteo y revision de humedad. Si la temperatura permanece por debajo de 35 C durante tres dias se evalua la composicion.

concepts.title: Volteo y aireacion
concepts.description: Frecuencia y tecnica para renovar el oxigeno sin compactar la pila.

sections.heading: Frecuencia de trabajo
sections.text: Durante el primer mes la pila se voltea cada siete dias. Mas adelante la frecuencia se ajusta a catorce dias si no aparecen olores ni zonas compactadas.

sections.heading: Distribucion del material
sections.text: El material exterior pasa al centro y el nucleo se distribuye hacia los bordes. Los bloques compactos se desarman sin moler las fibras.

concepts.title: Manejo de contaminacion y plagas
concepts.description: Acciones seguras ante impropios, insectos y presencia de roedores.

sections.heading: Retiro de impropios
sections.text: Los objetos visibles se retiran con guantes y se contabilizan por tipo. Una dispersion extensa de vidrio suspende la manipulacion manual de esa pila.

sections.heading: Senales de fauna
sections.text: Madrigueras, huellas o alimento expuesto requieren cubrir la superficie con quince centimetros de material seco y revisar el cierre perimetral.

concepts.title: Maduracion y control del lote
concepts.description: Criterios observables para determinar si un lote termino su fase activa.

sections.heading: Indicadores de madurez
sections.text: El material maduro tiene color oscuro, textura suelta y olor a suelo. Su temperatura se mantiene proxima a la ambiental durante siete dias.

sections.heading: Prueba de estabilidad
sections.text: Una muestra humedecida se conserva cerrada durante cuarenta y ocho horas. Un olor acido intenso o un aumento apreciable de temperatura prolonga la maduracion.

concepts.title: Entrega de compost a la comunidad
concepts.description: Preparacion de partidas y limites de uso comunicados a las personas receptoras.

sections.heading: Preparacion de partidas
sections.text: El compost se tamiza a doce milimetros y se entrega en recipientes limpios. Cada partida conserva fecha, peso y codigo de lote.

sections.heading: Indicaciones de uso
sections.text: Se recomienda mezclar una parte de compost con tres partes de suelo. El producto no se aplica directamente sobre semillas recien germinadas.

concepts.title: Boat intake assessment
concepts.description: Initial inspection criteria for rowing shells arriving at the workshop.

sections.heading: Safe support
sections.text: The shell is placed on padded stands beneath reinforced areas. Loose fittings are bagged and labeled before the hull is rotated.

sections.heading: Condition record
sections.text: The intake record includes overall photographs, visible cracks, previous repairs, and measured hull mass. Active water leakage keeps the boat out of routine work.

concepts.title: Timber moisture assessment
concepts.description: Moisture checks for wooden components before shaping or bonding.

sections.heading: Sampling locations
sections.text: Readings are taken at both ends and the center of each component. The meter pins avoid finished faces and existing fastener holes.

sections.heading: Working range
sections.text: Timber may be shaped between 8% and 12% moisture content. A difference greater than three points between locations requires additional conditioning.

concepts.title: Steaming replacement ribs
concepts.description: Controlled heating and forming of thin wooden ribs.

sections.heading: Steam duration
sections.text: A rib is steamed for one minute per millimeter of thickness. The chamber remains above 94 C without allowing the timber to contact standing water.

sections.heading: Forming window
sections.text: The rib reaches the mold within forty-five seconds of leaving the chamber. Clamps apply gradual pressure from the center toward both ends.

concepts.title: Epoxy bonding of structural repairs
concepts.description: Mixing, application, and curing conditions for structural adhesive joints.

sections.heading: Mixing control
sections.text: Resin and hardener are measured by mass at the manufacturer-approved ratio. Material scraped from the sides of the mixing cup is blended before application.

sections.heading: Curing conditions
sections.text: A bonded joint remains undisturbed for sixteen hours above 18 C. Clamps are removed only after the adhesive surface is firm and no longer tacky.

concepts.title: Balancing repaired oars
concepts.description: Checks for mass distribution and alignment after an oar repair.

sections.heading: Balance point
sections.text: The balance point is measured from the handle end with all fittings installed. Paired oars may differ by no more than eight millimeters.

sections.heading: Blade alignment
sections.text: The blade face is checked against a straight reference surface. Twist greater than one degree across the blade requires correction.

concepts.title: Protective coating application
concepts.description: Surface preparation and coating limits for repaired wooden parts.

sections.heading: Surface preparation
sections.text: The surface is sanded to 180 grit and cleared with filtered air. Dust, wax, and uncured adhesive prevent coating application.

sections.heading: Layer schedule
sections.text: Three thin coats are applied with at least six hours between layers. The finished part cures for forty-eight hours before water exposure.

concepts.title: Post-repair lake trial
concepts.description: Operational checks performed during a controlled return to the water.

sections.heading: Trial conditions
sections.text: The trial takes place in sheltered water with wind below 12 knots. A support launch remains within visual range.

sections.heading: Observed behavior
sections.text: The crew records tracking, unusual vibration, water entry, and fitting movement. Any new crack or persistent leak ends the trial immediately.

concepts.title: Workshop tool calibration
concepts.description: Routine verification of measuring and clamping equipment.

sections.heading: Verification schedule
sections.text: Scales, torque tools, and moisture meters are checked every ninety days. A tool is also checked after impact or an unexplained reading.

sections.heading: Out-of-tolerance response
sections.text: A failed tool is tagged and removed from use. Work completed since its last valid check is reviewed for possible remeasurement.

concepts.title: Seed donation intake
concepts.description: Information and physical checks required when donated seed arrives.

sections.heading: Required details
sections.text: Each donation records the crop, variety name, harvest season, growing area, and donor declaration. Seed without a known crop identity remains separated.

sections.heading: Initial condition
sections.text: The container is checked for moisture, insects, mold, and mixed material. Damp or visibly affected seed is isolated from the active collection.

concepts.title: Cleaning small seed lots
concepts.description: Mechanical separation of seed from chaff and foreign material.

sections.heading: Cleaning sequence
sections.text: Coarse material is removed by hand before the lot passes through nested screens. Air separation uses the lowest flow that moves chaff without carrying sound seed.

sections.heading: Loss control
sections.text: Rejected material is inspected before disposal. A loss greater than 10% of the incoming mass triggers a second manual sort.

concepts.title: Drying and moisture verification
concepts.description: Low-temperature drying and release limits for cleaned seed.

sections.heading: Drying method
sections.text: Seed is spread in a single layer in moving room-temperature air. Direct sun and heated air above 30 C are avoided.

sections.heading: Release check
sections.text: Two samples taken twelve hours apart must both read at or below 8% moisture. A rising second result extends the drying period.

concepts.title: Germination sampling
concepts.description: Small-lot test for estimating viability without consuming excessive seed.

sections.heading: Sample setup
sections.text: Twenty seeds are placed on evenly moistened paper in a covered tray. The tray carries the lot code and start date.

sections.heading: Result classification
sections.text: Normal seedlings are counted at the crop-specific observation day. A result below 70% is marked low viability and receives a reduced storage interval.

concepts.title: Packet labeling
concepts.description: Minimum information printed on packets prepared for circulation.

sections.heading: Label fields
sections.text: The packet shows crop, variety, lot code, harvest season, quantity, and viability status. Handwritten corrections are initialed and dated.

sections.heading: Plain-language notes
sections.text: Special isolation or sowing notes use complete words rather than internal abbreviations. Unverified traits are not presented as established characteristics.

concepts.title: Cold cabinet storage
concepts.description: Environmental limits and placement rules for sealed seed packets.

sections.heading: Cabinet conditions
sections.text: The cabinet operates between 4 C and 8 C with relative humidity below 40%. Readings are recorded twice each day.

sections.heading: Packet placement
sections.text: Packets remain in sealed boxes above the cabinet floor. Lots with different status labels occupy separate trays.

concepts.title: Seed checkout and return
concepts.description: Circulation records and handling of seed returned by members.

sections.heading: Checkout record
sections.text: A checkout records the packet lot, quantity, member code, and date. The transaction does not include personal growing notes.

sections.heading: Returned seed
sections.text: Returned seed is treated as a new donation unless its sealed packet is intact. Open packets never re-enter the original lot.

concepts.title: Seasonal inventory review
concepts.description: Periodic checks for quantity, age, condition, and record consistency.

sections.heading: Physical count
sections.text: Each packet is counted against its recorded quantity band and storage location. Missing or duplicate labels are isolated for correction.

sections.heading: Seasonal action
sections.text: Old, low-viability, or damaged lots are marked for retesting or removal. No lot is discarded until its status change is recorded.
